description: Close documentation gaps from the recent 2-day release cluster (v0.5.28 through v0.5.37). Audit found three user-facing user-visible gaps where ship code outpaced the user-facing docs: file-claim locks (v0.5.30) absent from docs/commands.md, git.prefer_resumable / git.resume_stale_threshold knobs (v0.5.33) absent from docs/commands.md, README Workflow Features table not reflecting recent capabilities. CLAUDE.md already covers all three internally; this work is a docs-only catchup so external users find the same information their internal counterparts already have.

userStories.title: Document touches_files / file-claim locks in docs/commands.md
userStories.description:
As a user driving claw-forge run with parallel agents, I need to know how to declare file-edit intent so two agents don't step on the same file. As a user using the state-service REST API directly, I need to know that POST /sessions/{id}/tasks accepts a touches_files field and that POST /sessions/{id}/file-claims is the underlying lock-acquire endpoint.

Add a subsection (probably under `claw-forge run` near the existing concurrency notes) titled something like 'Parallel-safe agents via file-claim locks'. Cover:

1. WHAT it is — atomic per-session file locks the dispatcher acquires before starting an agent. Tasks declare `touches_files: list[str]` on creation; if any file is already claimed, the dispatcher defers the task to the next dispatch cycle.
2. HOW to opt in — pass `touches_files` when POSTing to /sessions/{id}/tasks. The list contains repo-relative paths. Tasks WITHOUT touches_files participate in NO locking (full backward compat).
3. AUTO-RELEASE — claims drop on task transition to completed/failed/paused. A forgotten release can't deadlock other tasks.
4. RELATIONSHIP to merge-gating (`merged_to_target_branch`) — file-claims handle SPATIAL conflicts (same file, same time); merge-gating handles TEMPORAL conflicts (dependent task waits for parent's squash to land). The two are independent and complementary.
5. ENDPOINTS — list the three file-claim endpoints from CLAUDE.md (POST/DELETE/GET on /sessions/{id}/file-claims) for users hitting the API directly.

The content is already in CLAUDE.md (search for 'File-claim locks' and 'touches_files'). Translate it into user-facing prose appropriate for docs/commands.md (which is more example-driven and less architecture-driven than CLAUDE.md). Use the same example shape as nearby sections.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/commands.md has a new subsection covering touches_files / file-claim locks under claw-forge run (or in a logical adjacent location)
Subsection explains what file-claims are, how to opt in via touches_files, auto-release behavior, and relationship to merge-gating
All three file-claim API endpoints (POST /sessions/{id}/file-claims, DELETE /sessions/{id}/file-claims/{task_id}, GET /sessions/{id}/file-claims) are mentioned
Tasks without touches_files participate in no locking is explicitly stated (backward compat)
Coverage gate still holds (no code changes, so no test impact)
Lint passes (uv run ruff check claw_forge/ tests/) — should be unchanged
Markdown lint clean (no broken syntax, headings nest correctly)
userStories.notes: Source material in CLAUDE.md sections about file-claim locks, touches_files, and the State Service API endpoint list. Docs/commands.md currently has 0 hits for these terms (verified via grep). Target file: docs/commands.md only.

userStories.title: Document git.prefer_resumable + git.resume_stale_threshold in docs/commands.md
userStories.description:
As a user tuning claw-forge for their workload, I need to know that resume scheduling is configurable, what the knobs do, and what the defaults are.

Add to docs/commands.md (under `claw-forge run` config notes, alongside the existing `git.cleanup_orphan_worktrees` and `git.llm_conflict_proposals` documentation around line 1582) a short section covering:

1. `git.prefer_resumable: true` (default true) — at equal priority, the scheduler prefers tasks whose feature branch has committed work. Disabling forces fresh starts every time.
2. `git.resume_stale_threshold: 50` (default 50) — when target_branch has moved more than N commits ahead of the feature branch, skip the resume preference. A coarse 'resuming will likely conflict' heuristic.
3. Show a YAML snippet under git: with both knobs set explicitly so users can copy-paste.
4. Reference the new pre-dispatch sync (already documented as resume_conflict failure mode) — note that even with prefer_resumable disabled, the sync still runs at dispatch time as a final guardrail.

Keep this short — the depth of explanation should match the existing `git.cleanup_orphan_worktrees` documentation in the same area.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/commands.md mentions both git.prefer_resumable and git.resume_stale_threshold with their default values
A YAML snippet shows the knobs configured in claw-forge.yaml
Each knob has a one-sentence explanation of what disabling/lowering/raising it does
Section sits adjacent to the existing git config knobs (around the smart-mode cleanup section near line 1582)
Lint passes (uv run ruff check claw_forge/ tests/) — unchanged
userStories.notes: Source material in CLAUDE.md 'Resume preference' Key Conventions bullet. The knobs are read in cli.py:781-783. Target file: docs/commands.md only.

userStories.title: Add Workflow Features table rows in README.md for recent user-visible capabilities
userStories.description:
As someone landing on the README to understand what claw-forge does, I should see the recent user-visible capabilities reflected in the 'Workflow Features' table at line 628.

The table currently lists YOLO mode, fix, pause/resume, human input, status, batch features, pool health. It does NOT list:

1. **Parallel-safe agents via touches_files** (v0.5.30) — Command/flag column: `touches_files: [...]` in task creation payload (or POST /sessions/{id}/file-claims for direct API users)
2. **Reset failed/blocked tasks in bulk** (v0.5.32) — Command/flag column: 'Reset All' button on Failed/Blocked column headers in Kanban UI
3. **Resume-conflict pre-dispatch surfacing** (v0.5.37) — Command/flag column: automatic — the resume_conflict task failure surfaces in the Kanban UI with the file list; recovery is `cd .claw-forge/worktrees/<slug>` + manual git merge

Add these as 3 new rows to the existing markdown table. Keep the formatting exactly consistent with the existing rows (same column widths-ish, same tone). Do NOT restructure the table or add new columns.

If any of these features feel too internal/edge-case for the headline 'Workflow Features' table, optionally place them in the 'Quick Workflow Cheatsheet' section instead — but at minimum the parallel-safe-via-touches_files row should be in Workflow Features because it's a major value prop for the parallelism story.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md 'Workflow Features' table has at least 2 new rows covering recent capabilities (touches_files, Reset All, OR resume_conflict)
All new rows preserve the existing table format (3 columns: Feature / Command / Flag)
No restructuring of the existing table — only additions
Rendered markdown displays correctly (table not broken)
Lint passes (uv run ruff check claw_forge/ tests/) — unchanged
userStories.notes: Target file: README.md only. The table starts at line 628. Cross-reference: docs/commands.md should already have these features covered after US-001 and US-002.
